questions.stem: Given unity feedback system with G(s) = K(s+5) / [s(s+1.2)(s+3)], find the range of K that keeps the system stable.
common_errors: Arithmetic error building Routh array rows
Forgetting to include the zero-gain boundary (K=0) in the range
Sign error in characteristic equation from unity feedback
questions.solution_method: Form closed-loop CE: s(s+1.2)(s+3) + K(s+5) = 0. Expand, build 4-row Routh array. Require all first-column entries > 0 to find the K range.
key_formulas: Routh stability: all first-column signs positive

questions.stem: For the same system G(s) = K(s+5) / [s(s+1.2)(s+3)] with unity feedback, find the value of K that makes the system oscillate.
common_errors: Using the wrong row to form the auxiliary equation
Solving for K from the wrong Routh row
questions.solution_method: Set the s^1 row of the Routh array to zero and solve for K (marginal stability condition). This gives K_marginal.

questions.stem: For the same system, find the frequency of oscillation when K is set to the value that makes the system oscillate.
common_errors: Using the s^1 row instead of the auxiliary polynomial from the s^2 row
Forgetting to take sqrt to get omega from omega^2
questions.solution_method: Substitute K_marginal into the s^2 row to form the auxiliary polynomial A(s) = as^2 + b = 0. Solve for s = ±jω to get the oscillation frequency ω.

questions.stem: For the polynomial S^6 + 3s^5 + 4s^4 + 6s^3 + 5s^2 + 3s + 2, determine the number of poles in the LHP, RHP, and on the imaginary axis. Determine the values of all poles and indicate their locations in the s-plane.
common_errors: Mishandling zero in first column — failing to use epsilon method or auxiliary polynomial
Incorrect sign-change count after epsilon substitution
Not factoring out auxiliary polynomial to find imaginary-axis poles
Arithmetic errors in 6th-order Routh array
questions.solution_method: Build the 7-row Routh array. Handle any special cases (zero in first column or zero row). Count sign changes in first column for RHP poles. Use auxiliary polynomial when a zero row appears to find imaginary-axis poles. Factor the characteristic polynomial or use the auxiliary polynomial roots for exact pole values.
key_formulas: # RHP poles = sign changes in first column
Auxiliary poly A(s) from zero-row: A(s)=0 gives imaginary-axis poles

questions.stem: Find the value of K in the system of Fig. Q2(a) (which includes blocks K/s^2, 2/s, 1/(s+1), and an input pre-filter s^2) that will place the closed-loop poles at the locations shown in Fig. Q2(b) (poles at -1 ± j1 and a real pole, visible on s-plane sketch).
common_errors: Incorrect block diagram reduction leading to wrong open-loop TF
Using angle condition without verifying the point lies on the root locus
Arithmetic error computing |G(s)H(s)| at the desired pole
questions.solution_method: Reduce the block diagram to find the closed-loop characteristic equation as a function of K. Substitute the desired closed-loop pole locations (read from Fig. Q2(b)) into the CE and solve for K using the magnitude condition |G(s_d)H(s_d)| = 1.

questions.stem: For the closed-loop system with G_c(s) = (s+α)/(s+4) and G(s) = K/[s(s+2)(s+4)] in unity feedback (Fig. Q3), determine the values of α and K for the overall system to be stable.
common_errors: Not simplifying (s+4) cancellation in G_c(s)·G(s) before forming CE
Treating α as fixed when building Routh array — must keep it symbolic
Inequalities wrong direction when solving simultaneous Routh conditions
questions.solution_method: Compute open-loop TF G_c(s)G(s) = K(s+α)/[s(s+2)(s+4)^2] noting the (s+4) in G_c denominator does not cancel with G(s)'s (s+4). Form CE = s(s+2)(s+4)^2 + K(s+α) = 0, expand, build Routh array with symbolic K and α. Apply all first-column > 0 conditions to find the stability region in (α, K) space.

questions.stem: For the same system (Fig. Q3), suggest suitable values of α and K while assuring that the steady-state error due to a unit ramp input is no more than 20%.
common_errors: Using Kv formula without accounting for the pole-zero cancellation structure
Selecting (α, K) satisfying sse ≤ 0.2 but forgetting to verify stability
Confusing 20% steady-state error with e_ss ≤ 0.2 vs e_ss ≤ 0.2·ramp-slope
questions.solution_method: From part (a), identify the stability region. Compute velocity constant Kv = lim_{s→0} s·G_c(s)G(s) = Kα/32. For unit ramp, e_ss = 1/Kv ≤ 0.20 → Kα ≥ 160. Pick (α, K) satisfying Kα ≥ 160 within the stable region and verify via Routh.

notes: Q3 block diagram involves a forward-path pre-filter s^2 and multiple inner loops; exact pole locations in Fig. Q2(b) show two complex poles at approximately -1±j1 and one real pole near -2, inferred visually from the s-plane sketch.
